2604.15302.title: Diagnosing LLM Judge Reliability: Conformal Prediction Sets and Transitivity Violations
2604.15302.paper_summary: LLM-as-judge frameworks are increasingly used for automatic NLG evaluation, yet their per-instance reliability remains poorly understood. We present a two-pronged diagnostic toolkit applied to SummEval: $\textbf{(1)}$ a transitivity analysis that reveals widespread per-input inconsistency masked by low aggregate violation rates ($\barρ = 0.8$-$4.1\%$), with $33$-$67\%$ of documents exhibiting at least one directed 3-cycle; and $\textbf{(2)}$ split conformal prediction sets over 1-5 Likert scores providing theoretically-guaranteed $\geq(1{-}α)$ coverage, with set width serving as a per-instance reliability indicator ($r_s = {+}0.576$, $N{=}1{,}918$, $p < 10^{-100}$, pooled across all judges). Critically, prediction set width shows consistent cross-judge agreement ($\bar{r} = 0.32$-$0.38$), demonstrating it captures document-level difficulty rather than judge-specific noise. Across four judges and four criteria, both diagnostics converge: criterion matters more than judge, with relevance judged most reliably (avg. set size $\approx 3.0$) and coherence moderately so (avg. set size $\approx 3.9$), while fluency and consistency remain unreliable (avg. set size $\approx 4.9$). We release all code, prompts, and cached results.
2604.15302.paper_summary_zh: LLM-as-judge 框架越來越多地被用於自動 NLG 評估，但它們的每個實例可靠性仍然不甚了解。<lb/>我們展示了一個應用於 SummEval 的雙管齊下的診斷工具包：$\textbf{(1)}$ 一個傳遞性分析，揭示了被低總體違規率 ($\barρ = 0.8$-$4.1\%$) 掩蓋的普遍每個輸入不一致性，其中 $33$-$67\%$ 的文檔顯示至少有一個定向的 3-循環；以及 $\textbf{(2)}$ 在 1-5 Likert 分數上分割的符合預測集，提供理論上保證的 $\geq(1{-}α)$ 覆蓋率，集合寬度作為每個實例的可靠性指標 ($r_s = {+}0.576$, $N{=}1{,}918$, $p < 10^{-100}$，在所有評審中合併)。<lb/>關鍵是，預測集寬度顯示出一致的跨評審一致性 ($\bar{r} = 0.32$-$0.38$)，表明它捕捉了文檔級別的難度，而不是評審特定的噪音。<lb/>在四位評審和四個標準中，這兩個診斷結果趨於一致：標準比評審更重要，相關性被評判得最可靠（平均集合大小 $\approx 3.0$）而連貫性則適度（平均集合大小 $\approx 3.9$），而流暢性和一致性仍然不可靠（平均集合大小 $\approx 4.9$）。<lb/>我們釋放所有代碼、提示和緩存結果。
2604.15302.author: Manan Gupta et.al.
